senses.text: "to make a moaning or murmuring sound," Old English swogan "to sound, roar, howl, rustle, whistle," from Proto-Germanic *swoganan (source also of Old Saxon swogan "to rustle," Gothic gaswogjan "to sigh"), from PIE imitative root *(s)wagh- (source also of Greek echo, Latin vagire "to cry, roar, sound"). The noun is late 14c., from the verb.
